rulings.Case Title: 3H RENOVATION SERVICES, LLC ET AL VS. BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANTs FAY SERVICING, LLC, ANH PHAM, BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC., and SKYGROUP REALTY, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend. No opposition filed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF DAMIEN QUINN's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings GRANTED. Motion is granted as to the to 17th cause of action without leave to amend. No substantive opposition filed. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend as to the 18th cause of action to state facts in support of each element of the cause of action for abuse of process under the circumstances of this case, including wrongful use of process after it is issued. The Court notes that the moving party chose (no indication in the reply declaration that failure to comply was a result of inadvertence/mistake) not to comply with CCP 439 (no declaration filed and arguments made in meet and confer discussions are different from the arguments advanced in the motion, e.g. 17th cause of action) and CRC 3.1110. The Court will take motions off calendar in the future for failure to comply with any procedural requirements of CCP, CRC and SFLR. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JANET TAPIA VS. MARINA E MCDANIEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Janet Tapia'S Motion To Allow A Complaint To Be Filed Against An Attorney Containing A Cause Of Action For Civil Conspiracy With A Client
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2019 line 3. Plaintiff Janet Tapia'S Motion To Allow A Complaint To Be Filed Against An Attorney Containing A Cause Of Action For Civil Conspiracy With A Client OFF CALENDAR. Moving party failed to file a verified petition. If Plaintiff files a properly verified petition it shall be served on the defendant and calendared at least 16 court days out. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KALPESHKUMAR GOHIL VS. TONY LAMARR BLU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.5
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2019 line 4. PLAINTIFF KALPESHKUMAR GOHIL's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To CCP 128.5 DENIED, inter alia, for failure to comply with CCP 128.5 safe harbor provision without any explanation. San Diegans for Open Government v. City of San Diego (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 1306 was superseded by 2017 statute as recognized in Nutrition Distribution, LLC v. Southern SARMs, Inc. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 117. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. RHONDA PATTERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT RHONDA PATTERSON's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD GRANTED. Counsel to prepare order. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY LEON LOPEZ VS. ERNEST LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANT ERNEST LOPEZ's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states sufficient facts to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on termination of license and is not uncertain. = (501/CFH)
